lecture_point: A model produces a number every day, such as Black-Scholes theoretical value versus market premium
implementation_rule: Use the model continuously, but know where its assumptions fail
failure_mode: A model can look good while the market prices a risk the model omits

lecture_point: Earnings, rate decisions, and other triggers create option opportunities
implementation_rule: Use event trades as special cases, not the only source of alpha
failure_mode: The event may not be the only reason price moves, and events are hard to scale

lecture_point: A trader can use a t-stat near 1, not only the academic t-stat near 2
implementation_rule: Require many independent bets before trusting a 55% edge
failure_mode: Small edge without enough trade count is still noise

lecture_point: The good limit orders may not fill, while the bad ones fill immediately
implementation_rule: Model fill probability and do not count unfilled winners as live P&L
failure_mode: Backtest assumes every signal fills and overstates edge

lecture_point: Bid-ask, brokerage, taxes, platform, and exchange fees can erase 20-30% or more of gross profit
implementation_rule: Subtract costs inside the backtest, not after the fact
failure_mode: Gross Sharpe survives but net CAGR turns negative
